message:
fix(stdlib): strtod -NaN sign bit via IEEE 754 bit manipulation

The previous implementation used `special_sign * f64::NAN` to produce
negative NaN, but IEEE 754 §6.3 says the sign of a NaN result from
arithmetic is implementation-defined. On some platforms the
multiplication doesn't preserve the sign bit.

Now uses direct bit manipulation: f64::from_bits(NAN.to_bits() | 1<<63)
to reliably set the sign bit for -NaN, which is the only portable
way to produce a negative NaN per IEEE 754 sign-bit semantics.

Adds test_strtod_negative_nan() verifying the sign bit is set and
that 4 bytes are consumed for "-nan".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
